coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Saturday long run: 14–16 miles, Z2 aerobic only (140–150 bpm), with 800–1200 ft of vert built in naturally (rolling terrain preferred). You're in taper week 20 with volume already down 32% from last week—this is your last meaningful long effort before final sharpening. Keep it conversational, sub-threshold. Your resting HR is 53 bpm and recovery score is 73/100, so you're genuinely recovered; use this window. Avoid the temptation to test pace; the Julian Alps will demand patience, not speed, on race day.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Front-load carbs this morning: 60–80g (oats, banana, toast, honey) 90 min pre-run. On the run, take 200–300 calories per hour after the first 60 min—mix gels (30g carbs each) with real food or sports drink to hit 60–80g carbs/hour and keep sodium at 500–700 mg/hour. Post-run within 30 min: 40g protein + 80–100g carbs to cap glycogen repletion. You're 97 days out; alpine efforts demand stable fuel economy, and taper is when you dial the specifics in rather than experiment.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Sleep last night was 6.54 hours at 88.4% efficiency—solid, above your 30-day average of 5.13 hours. Protect tonight: aim for 7.5–8 hours by getting horizontal by 10:30 pm. Taper phase is when sleep quality compounds; prioritize it more than volume. Your resting HR dropped 8 bpm (61.1 → 53 bpm average), which signals real adaptation. Cool-down walk 10–15 min post-run today, then easy mobility or foam roll for hip and calf (alpine terrain will demand ankle/calf stability). No ice bath needed; active recovery is enough.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're at week 20 of a 22-week cycle with 97 days to Kranjska Gora. The hard work is done. Taper mind games are real—you'll feel sluggish around week 21, and that's normal. Your current recovery data shows you're primed, not overtrained. Use this run to reconnect with the terrain feel and pacing you'll need in Slovenia (steady climbs, minimal power, high Z2 time). Don't chase splits. Come next week, one final sharpening block, then you trust the taper and show up race-ready.
